source.label: Scientific plotting template collection 1-124
source.use: Visual archetypes were audited and transformed into original scientific workflows. Source artwork and proprietary Prism files are not redistributed.

families.contract: one row per biological unit with time, event indicator, group, and unit ID
families.upgrade: Preserve censoring and risk sets; do not infer Cox effects from a decorative curve.

families.contract: sample, category, non-negative value, and optional group
families.upgrade: Replace donuts and decorative stacks with comparable sample-level compositions and enforce positive totals.

families.contract: continuous value, group, biological-unit ID, design, and authoritative order
candidates: raincloud or violin when sample size supports density
families.upgrade: Show biological-unit observations and estimates; remove watercolor, flower, card, and bar-only decoration.

families.contract: time, value, group, and biological-unit ID
families.upgrade: Retain within-unit structure and uncertainty; do not hide replication behind smoothed decorative lines.

families.contract: unique row, column, and numeric value cells
families.upgrade: Record ordering and clustering; use a centered diverging scale only when values cross zero.

families.contract: two conditions, continuous value, and stable paired biological-unit ID
families.upgrade: Replace bars and dumbbells detached from raw pairs with explicit matched trajectories and effect intervals.

families.contract: numeric x, numeric y, one row per biological unit, and optional group
families.upgrade: Record Pearson, Spearman, and slope as association; require a declared nonlinear model for spline claims.

families.contract: term, estimate, lower CI, upper CI, and optional group
families.upgrade: Reserve a label/value column and never print values over intervals.

families.contract: feature, effect size, and adjusted P value
families.upgrade: Use adjusted P values, explicit thresholds, restrained highlighting, and collision-free hit labels.

families.contract: one row per independent case with observed and predicted class
families.upgrade: Show denominators and class order; do not use color intensity without cell values.

families.contract: term, effect or enrichment score, adjusted P value, count, and optional ontology
families.upgrade: Encode effect, evidence, and count separately; limit displayed terms using a declared ranking rule.

families.contract: positive dose, response, group, and biological-unit ID when replicates exist
candidates: four-parameter logistic fit with EC50 or IC50 interval
families.upgrade: Fit on the log-dose scale, retain observations, and write 50 as a scientific subscript.

families.contract: binary outcome, continuous score, biological-unit ID, and optional cohort
families.upgrade: Preserve independent cases and prevalence; do not present internally tuned performance as external validation.

families.contract: feature and numeric score with optional interval and class
families.upgrade: Use a declared ordering rule and label only a readable number of features.

families.contract: precomputed embedding x, embedding y, and group label
families.upgrade: Visualize supplied coordinates without inventing clusters or relabeling cell states.

families.contract: shared x plus two numeric outcomes and optional group
candidates: vertically aligned panels with shared x
families.upgrade: Do not use arbitrary dual-axis scaling; show native units in aligned panels or compare standardized trajectories explicitly.

families.contract: item, signed value, and optional group
families.upgrade: Use a meaningful zero and stable left/right semantics; avoid mirrored decoration without a shared scale.

families.contract: immutable assay image, lane/sample map, quantitative table, biological-unit ID, and calibration or normalization rule
candidates: standardized assay image plus aligned quantification
matrix or distribution view of quantified signal
families.upgrade: Keep raw blots immutable, align lanes with quantification, disclose normalization, and remove watercolor styling.

families.contract: continuous value, group, and biological-unit ID
families.upgrade: Use empirical steps rather than decorative smoothing and retain sample counts.

families.contract: unit, start, end, status, optional event time, and optional group
families.upgrade: Keep censoring/status symbols explicit and sort by a declared clinical rule.
